zh.Ennis, Clare, Munster, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 克萊爾恩尼斯
zh.Ennis Friary, Ennis, Clare, Munster, Ire: 愛爾蘭庫迪 克萊爾恩尼斯恩尼斯 1
zh.lifeSummary: Éadaoin Nic Gormáin Queen of Thomond於1285年出生在愛爾蘭。她於1300年與Muircheartach Ó Briain King of Thomond在愛爾蘭庫迪 克萊爾恩尼斯結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1367年在愛爾蘭庫迪 克萊爾恩尼斯享年82歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 克萊爾恩尼斯恩尼斯 1。

zh-hans.Ennis, Clare, Munster, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 克萊爾恩尼斯
zh-hans.Ennis Friary, Ennis, Clare, Munster, Ire: 爱尔兰庫迪 克萊爾恩尼斯恩尼斯 1
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Éadaoin Nic Gormáin Queen of Thomond于1285年出生在爱尔兰。她于1300年与Muircheartach Ó Briain King of Thomond在爱尔兰庫迪 克萊爾恩尼斯结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1367年在爱尔兰庫迪 克萊爾恩尼斯享年82岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 克萊爾恩尼斯恩尼斯 1。

nameMeanings.name: Nic
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Nick, a pet form of Nichol and Nicholas .
South German: from a pet form of the personal name Nikolaus (see Nicholas ).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Negus
meanings: English: perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Nikecus, Nikus (a pet fom of Nicol(e), male or female) + the hypcoristic suffix -cus. See Nick , Nichol . In the modern form of this name, the voicing of /k/ to /g/ may have been encouraged by false association with the word Negus meaning ‘emperor of Ethiopia’ (see 3 below), which appears first in 1594 and 1613 in East Anglian authors as neguz.

Romanian (Neguş): from Neguş, a diminutive of the personal name Neagu, a short form of any of various South Slavic personal names composed of the element neg ‘to take care of, to nurse’, e.g. Negomir (Romanian Neagomir).

Ethiopian: from the personal name Negus, meaning e.g. ‘king’ in the Amharic language. — Note: Since Ethiopians do not have hereditary surnames, this name was registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Neck
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English necke ‘neck’ (Old English hnecca), denoting someone with some peculiarity of the neck. Symon Chortneke, i.e. ‘short neck’, is probably identical with Symon Nekeles, i.e. ‘neckless’, both recorded in 1275 in the Norfolk Hundred Rolls.
English: perhaps also a variant of   Nick  .
German: from Neck, the name of a water sprite.

variants.name: Nichol
meanings: Scottish and northern English: from the Middle English personal name Nic(h)ol(e), a vernacular form of Nic(h)olas (see Nicholas ). Compare Nickel and Nicol .

In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Greek Nikolas , its variant Nicolas , or of any of various derivatives of the personal name Nikola(o)s ‘Nicholas’, including Nikolis (see Nicoli ). Compare Nicholas and Nicol .

variants.name: Nickens
meanings: Apparently a patronymic from a pet form of   Nick  .

nameMeanings.name: Gormáin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Pierre, Armand, Jacques, Raoul, Wesner, Andre, Andree, Camil, Camille, Christophe, Dieudonne.

French and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Germain, Latin Germanus. This was popular in France, where it had been borne by a 5th-century Christian saint, bishop of Auxerre. It derives from Latin germanus ‘brother, cousin’ (originally an adjective meaning ‘of the same stock’, from Latin germen ‘bud, shoot’) or, alternatively, from the ethnic name Germanus ‘German’. In the Italian, the popularity of the equivalent personal name (see Germano ) has been enhanced by association with the meaning ‘brother (in God)’. This surname is most common in Haiti.
History: This surname is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Germano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Antonio, Domenico, Nino, Sal, Salvatore, Angelo, Carmello, Franco, Geno, Guido, Carmelo.
Italian and Portuguese: from the personal name Germano (see Germain ).

variants.name: Germaine
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Emile, Henri.
Altered form of French   Germain  .
French: from the feminine form of   Germain  . Compare   Germond  .

variants.name: German
meanings: English: ethnic name from Old French germain ‘German’ (from Latin Germanus). This sometimes denoted an actual immigrant from Germany, but was also used to refer to a person who had trade or other connections with German-speaking lands. The Latin word Germanus is of obscure and disputed origin; the most plausible of the etymologies that have been proposed is that the people were originally known as the ‘spear-men’, with ancient Germanic gēr, gār ‘spear’ as the first element.

Spanish and Hungarian (Germán); English (of Norman origin) and German: from the personal name Spanish and Hungarian Germán, Old French Germain, German German, from Latin Germanus (see Germain ), a Christian saint's name.
Americanized form of German   Germann   1, a name of ancient Germanic origin.

variants.name: Gherman
meanings: Romanian: from the personal name Gherman, Latin Germanus (see Germain ).

nameMeanings.name: Éadaoin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Modern form of   Étaín .

variants.name: Eadan
meanings: Modern form of   Étaín .

en.lifeSummary: Éadaoin Nic Gormáin Queen of Thomond was born in 1285, in Ireland. She married Muircheartach Ó Briain King of Thomond in 1300, in Ennis, County Clare, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1367, in Ennis, County Clare, Ireland, at the age of 82, and was buried in Ennis 1, Ennis, County Clare, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Éadaoin Nic Gormáin Queen of Thomond est née en 1285, à Irlande. Elle a épousé Muircheartach Ó Briain King of Thomond en 1300, à Ennis, County Clare, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée en 1367, à Ennis, County Clare, Irlande, à l’âge de 82 ans, et a été enterrée à Ennis 1, Ennis, County Clare, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Éadaoin Nic Gormáin Queen of Thomond wurde 1285 in Irland geboren. Sie hat 1300 in Ennis, County Clare, Irland Muircheartach Ó Briain King of Thomond geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb 1367 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Ennis, County Clare, Irland und wurde in Ennis 1, Ennis, County Clare, Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Éadaoin Nic Gormáin Queen of Thomond è nata il nel 1285 nel Irlanda. Lei sposato Muircheartach Ó Briain King of Thomond nel 1300, nel Ennis, County Clare, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1367, nel Ennis, County Clare, Irlanda, all'età di 82 anni, e fu sepolta a Ennis 1, Ennis, County Clare, Irlanda.

ja.Ennis, Clare, Munster, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ クラーエニス
ja.Ennis Friary, Ennis, Clare, Munster, Ire: アイルランドコウントイ クラーエニスエニス 1
ja.lifeSummary: Éadaoin Nic Gormáin Queen of Thomondは1285年にアイルランドで生まれた。1300年にアイルランドコウントイ クラーエニスでMuircheartach Ó Briain King of Thomondと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1367年にアイルランドコウントイ クラーエニスで82歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ クラーエニスエニス 1にて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Éadaoin Nic Gormáin Queen of Thomond(은)는 1285년 아일랜드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1300년 아일랜드 카운티 클래르 니스에서 Muircheartach Ó Briain King of Thomond(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1367년 아일랜드 카운티 클래르 니스에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 클래르 니스 니스 1에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Éadaoin Nic Gormáin Queen of Thomond nasceu em 1285, em Irlanda. Ela casou-se com Muircheartach Ó Briain King of Thomond em 1300, em Ennis, County Clare, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1367, em Ennis, County Clare, Irlanda, com 82 anos, e foi sepultada em Ennis 1, Ennis, County Clare, Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Éadaoin Nic Gormáin Queen of Thomond родилась в 1285 г. в местности Ирландия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Muircheartach Ó Briain King of Thomond в 1300 г. в местности эннис, коунти клар, Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в 1367 г. в местности эннис, коунти клар, Ирландия в возрасте 82 лет и была похоронена в местности эннис 1, эннис, коунти клар, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Éadaoin Nic Gormáin Queen of Thomond nació en 1285, en Irlanda. Se casó con Muircheartach Ó Briain King of Thomond en 1300, en Ennis, County Clare, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió en 1367, en Ennis, County Clare, Irlanda, a la edad de 82 años, y fue sepultada en Ennis 1, Ennis, County Clare, Irlanda.
